name: Chocolate and Vanilla Chip Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. Spray a large or two small cookie sheets with nonstick cooking spray.
2. Sift flour into a measuring cup and level off. In a medium bowl, combine flour, cocoa powder, and baking powder; mix well.
3. In a large bowl, cream together sugar, brown sugar, and margarine until light and fluffy. Add the flour mixture and beat until combined. Stir in vanilla chips.
4. With spray-coated hands, shape the dough into three 7-inch long rolls. Place the rolls at least 3 inches apart on the sprayed cookie sheet; flatten each roll to form a rectangle that is 3/4 inch thick, about 3 inches wide and 7 inches long.
5. Bake the rectangles for 22-28 minutes or until they are lightly golden brown and firm to the touch. Place the rectangles on wire racks to cool for 5 minutes.
6. Wipe the cookie sheet clean. With a serrated knife, cut each rectangle into 1/2 inch slices and place them, cut side up, back on the cookie sheet.
7. Bake the slices for an additional 6-8 minutes or until the top surface is slightly dry. Turn the cookies over and bake for another 6-8 minutes or until the top surface is slightly dry. Remove from the cookie sheets and cool completely on wire racks.
Serving size: 1 cookie
Calories: 70 (20 from fat, 2g total fat, 12g carbs, 50mg sodium)
Source: Pillsbury Fast and Healthy Cookbook, Nov/Dec 96
